$generated: authored at P4 from the P0 catalog — reviewed at P4.5 (R9)
$policy: freeze-and-diverge (§10.5): a point-in-time extraction of the donor squad's MCP-authoring posture. Donor evolution is NOT auto-backported.
$surface: This file is the ONE sanctioned generation surface (R9). The installer AUTHORS each seat's mcp_config from the P0 catalog + the baked patterns below and applies it via `--mcp-config-file`; it does NOT derive-from-live (chicken-and-egg on a fresh squad — there is no live config to mutate). Ship BROAD (the whole catalog per seat); the P0 prune sweep + the runtime measurement loop trim by MEASURED usage. NEVER ship a pre-pruned server set — the already-pruned outcome is earned, not installed.

$directives.{{...}}: an init hole; canonical value + source live in holes.tmpl.json.
$directives.$from-profile: at P4, resolve to the concrete `mcpServers` object produced by applying the named profile (see `profiles`) to the catalog.
$directives.$apply: ordered list of pattern names (see `patterns`) merged onto the profile base, in order.
$directives.$search-query-tool-only: the search-egress server's query tool name, resolved from the catalog. Every other tool on that server — notably its arbitrary-URL fetch tool — is dropped.
$directives.$profile-vocab: valid `seat.mcp-profile` values a seat row may carry: broad | broad-cold | broad-hardened | ambient.

catalog.$doc: the full per-account MCP server set probed at P0 (server-key -> launch config). This is the broad base for every non-ambient seat. Two roles inside it are load-bearing for the baked patterns and the constitution MEMORY clause:

memory-backend.$doc: the recall/save server (constitution MEMORY). Its server-key, endpoint, and tool-call names ARE the {{MEMORY_BACKEND_URL_API}} probe — the donor's loopback endpoint NEVER ships. Present-and-active in every memory-mounted profile; suppressed for this seat by memory-tool-mask on COLD-review profiles.

search-egress.$doc: the web-search server that ALSO exposes an arbitrary-URL fetch tool. search-egress-restriction keeps the query tool and drops the fetch tool — the outbound-GET channel a filesystem/command `--settings` deny profile cannot see.

memory-tool-mask.$doc: COLD-review hardening. A per-agent mcp_config merges OVER the ambient one, so an {"enabled": false} stub on the memory server suppresses it for this seat — makes the constitution's COLD-review exemption literally true (the seat carries no recalled prior conclusion into a fresh review). audit-drift asserts the mask is present when a seat's mcp-asserts include `memory-masked`.

search-egress-restriction.$doc: egress hardening for a code-probing seat. The stdio server's enabledTools arg is the REAL gate — a `--settings` deny profile cannot gate an in-process outbound GET. Fail-loud asserted right after authoring: a sparse catalog would emit a null/absent server and pass silently, so assert the server is a non-null object AND the fetch tool is gone before applying. audit-drift asserts the restriction when a seat's mcp-asserts include `search-egress-restricted`.

broad.$doc: every server the account exposes, memory active. Default for memory-mounted pipeline + curator seats. Research and documentation servers stay here by default (no dedicated research seat). Ship broad — prune by measured usage later.

broad-cold.$doc: broad catalog with memory suppressed — COLD-review posture (a binding review lens reviews with no recalled priors). audit-asserts: memory-masked.

broad-hardened.$doc: broad catalog with the search server's arbitrary-URL fetch tool stripped — for a seat that runs member-authorized code probes. Pairs with the hardened `--settings` deny profile (see deny-profiles.tmpl.json). audit-asserts: search-egress-restricted.

ambient.$doc: ambient-registry runtime: the per-agent mcp_config is INERT (the runtime reads {{RUNTIME_MCP_MOUNT}} instead). Emit an EMPTY per-agent mcpServers and, at apply time, assert every seat-critical catalog server is registered in the ambient mount — warn LOUDLY (non-fatal) on any miss, because no squad script owns that registry.

//seat_configs: generated from seat-manifest.yaml at P4 — do not hand-edit. One authored mcp_config per seat, profile-resolved from the row's `mcp-profile`; applied via `--mcp-config-file` (never derive-from-live on a fresh squad).
